---
title: "HBCUs, the G.I. Bill, and Black Educational Attainment"

status: "Working Paper"
year: 2026
order: 2

description: >
  This paper studies whether geographic access to historically Black
  colleges and universities shaped the educational effects of the
  World War II and Korean War G.I. Bills.

categories:
  - Economic History
  - Economics of Education
  - Race and Inequality

pdf: "../../files/papers/molligo-hbcu-gi-bill.pdf"

citation:
  type: article
  author:
    - family: Molligo
      given: Patrick A.
  title: "HBCUs, the G.I. Bill, and Black Educational Attainment"
  issued: 2026
---

::: {.paper-page-links}

[Download PDF](../../files/papers/molligo-hbcu-gi-bill.pdf){.btn .btn-primary}

:::

## Abstract

This paper examines whether access to historically Black colleges and universities affected Black veterans’ ability to use G.I. Bill educational benefits.

Replace this text with the current abstract.

## Results

The preliminary results indicate that G.I. Bill exposure had larger effects on college attendance and educational attainment in areas with greater access to HBCUs, although several estimates are imprecise.

## Citation

Molligo, Patrick A. “HBCUs, the G.I. Bill, and Black Educational Attainment.” Working paper, 2026.
